items.title: As a red-hot global stock market stumbles into bear territory, this Wall Street bull spots a dip worth buying
items.description: Tom Lee of Fundstrat says he’s buying the dip, and not scared off by Samsung’s losses

items.title: Alibaba just had its best day in 10 months. Is it time for China techs to catch up?
items.description: Alibaba on Wednesday saw its best single share-price gain in 10 months, as the broader Chinese tech sector revved higher after missing the rally propelling U.S., Korean and Taiwanese companies.

items.title: Iran war will leave an inflation scar on the U.S. through 2027, IMF says
items.description: The conflict with Iran hasn’t hurt the U.S. and global economies as much as expected, but it will leave a lasting legacy: Another bout of high inflation that won’t disappear quickly.

items.title: ‘She wants him gone’: My friend took in a homeless man as a caretaker. After 10 years, how can she evict him?
items.description: “He lived rent-free, helping her with health-related issues and other tasks.”

items.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
items.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

items.title: It can take as little as 5 minutes online to get prescribed a GLP-1 for weight loss. Why that’s risky.
items.description: The research suggests that these companies prioritize “quick prescriptions” over good care

items.title: I found a simple way to save money on frozen pizza. What’s your best money-saving trick?
Kevin Warsh plans to stop scripting the Fed’s next moves. It could trigger a wild ride for traders.
items.description: James Bullard, a former president of the St. Louis Federal Reserve, said Fed Chair Kevin Warsh could decide on policy changes without signaling to the market first, sparking volatility

items.title: It was the world’s hottest stock market. Now South Korea has entered bear territory.
items.description: The cumulative impact of several different narratives — competition, sustainability, excessive leverage, international outflows — has sapped the Korean market of its upward mobility since clocking up

items.title: Oil prices jump by the most in two months after Trump suggests U.S.-Iran cease-fire is over
items.description: Brent crude and West Texas Intermediate contracts climbed on President Donald Trump saying dealing with Tehran is “a waste of time”
